claim: Claude Code hooks have three levels: local pre-tool-use (deterministic bash commands to block), global hooks (user-level protection across device), and prompt hooks (LLM evaluates novel commands)
actionable: Set up hooks at all three levels: deterministic blocking for known-dangerous commands, global rules for device-wide protection, and prompt hooks as a safety net for novel commands.
source_title: Claude Code is Amazing... Until It DELETES Production

claim: Prompt hooks (non-deterministic LLM evaluation of every bash command) are slower but catch dangerous commands agents have never seen before - use as a last-ditch safety layer
actionable: Enable prompt hooks to catch novel dangerous commands, but encode them as deterministic rules once discovered to avoid repeated LLM overhead.
source_title: Claude Code is Amazing... Until It DELETES Production

claim: Path protection can be granularly configured with zero-access paths (completely blocked), read-only paths (no writes/deletes), and no-delete paths (prevent deletions only)
actionable: Use patterns.yaml to define zero-access paths for secrets/config, read-only paths for critical code, and no-delete paths for databases.
source_title: Claude Code is Amazing... Until It DELETES Production

claim: Pattern-based hook configuration (patterns.yaml) allows quick updates to dangerous command blocks without redeploying - agents can modify the patterns file to add new protections
actionable: Maintain a patterns.yaml file with ask, deny, read-only, and no-delete patterns that agents and humans can easily update.
source_title: Claude Code is Amazing... Until It DELETES Production

claim: One bad hallucination or misconstrued prompt can permanently destroy months of work in production systems - damage control is non-negotiable for autonomous agents
actionable: Treat hooks and safety measures as insurance; invest heavily in preventing catastrophic failures before they occur.
source_title: Claude Code is Amazing... Until It DELETES Production

claim: Cloud Code skills can automate the installation and configuration of hook systems across multiple codebases using interactive prompts (ask user question tool)
actionable: Create a reusable skill that walks through hook configuration for new projects, asking about Python vs TypeScript and global vs project-level setup.
source_title: Claude Code is Amazing... Until It DELETES Production

claim: Building trust with agents doesn't always require trust - you can use deterministic hook systems so agents literally cannot run critical destructive commands
actionable: Use hooks to enforce permission boundaries so agents can't execute dangerous commands regardless of what they try to do.
source_title: Claude Code is Amazing... Until It DELETES Production

claim: Ask patterns in hooks allow agents to ask for permission before executing potentially risky but not strictly dangerous commands (like deleting from production tables)
actionable: Use 'ask: true' patterns for operations that need human confirmation but aren't absolutely forbidden.
source_title: Claude Code is Amazing... Until It DELETES Production
